data.narrative:
Distributed Spectrum is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Alex Wulff, Ben Harpe, and Isaac Struhl, having raised $15M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector companies at seed and Series A stages, supplemented by two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight additional private peers including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression methodology uses the $15M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $38M. Peer-Median analysis across the five comparable companies—ranging from $60M (Tenna Systems, seed stage) to $29.0B (Scale AI, Series A)—produces a median valuation of $300M, though this range reflects substantial stage and scale variance within the defense tech sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $32M to $345M, with a median point of $169M. The wide spread reflects two primary uncertainties: the extreme outlier status of Scale AI's $29.0B valuation, which inflates the peer ceiling, and inherent stage misalignment within the comparable set. Stage-Progression implies a more conservative near-term entry valuation, while the peer median anchors higher assuming market-rate pricing for defense tech risk and growth. Key driver of range compression would be clarification of Distributed Spectrum's revenue traction and capital efficiency relative to the Seed cohort peers.
